description_long: This helmet features a domed, segmented black shell with subtle white speckling, reminiscent of lacquered metal. Two prominent, upward-curving golden antler-like ornaments are attached at the front, giving it a distinctive and traditional appearance. The lower rim is accented with layered brown and dark red bands, suggesting reinforced protection or decorative trim. The surface finish is matte with some slight gloss, and the helmet appears to be constructed primarily from metal, possibly with leather or fabric lining inside. There are no visible ventilation holes, visors, or modern retention systems, indicating a historical or ceremonial design. The overall form and ornamentation are evocative of a Japanese samurai kabuto helmet.
description: A black domed helmet with golden antler-like ornaments and layered brown trim, resembling a traditional samurai kabuto. The surface is matte with subtle white speckles and no visible modern features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the golden antler ornaments prominently and the layered trim at the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the helmet's dome shape and the profile of the antler ornament.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, uninterrupted black dome and the symmetrical placement of the antler ornaments.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the helmet's layered trim and the antler ornament from a different angle.
